free_text: Structure of (gamma,n) cross sections in Si-28, S-32,
The  natural  silicon, sulphur, and calcium samples
were used  as  the  targets,  all  three have the
shape of cylinder with the diameter of  40  mm  and
The multichannel method  of the reaction yield
measurement was  used  in  which  the  bremsstrahlung
end-point  energy  has  been changed automatically in
each cycle of electron acceleration in betatron  with
The  Penfold - Leiss  unfolding   method   with
the different  bins  was  used  for  obtaining  cross
section data from experimental yield curve.
By  means  of  direct registration  the  curves of the
photoneutron yields were measured from the thresholds
of the reaction (gamma,n) up to 30 MeV for Si-28,
S-32, and Ca-40. The (gamma,n) reaction cross
sections obtained are compared with the calculations
performed within the framework  of  the particle-hole
approach. It is shown that for the Si-28 and S-32 the
configurational splitting of  the giant resonance is
main effect that determines the  gross  structure  of
Absolute cross section  data  were  obtained using the
comparison procedure for thin targets (2 g/cm-2)
of element  studied  and  the  data for copper target
from the measurement of H.H.Thies et. al.
